title: Names of well-known C standard library macros and functions should be avoided as identifiers
description: Names of well-known C standard library macros and functions should not be used as identifiersAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misrabad-practicecertWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoDefining or declaring identifiers with the same names as well-known macros and functions from the C standard library has the potential to thoroughly confuse people who are unfamiliar with the code base, possibly leading them to introduce additional errors. Therefore, the names of well-known C standard library macros and functions should not be used as identifiers.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: int printf = 10; // Noncompliant: shadows stdio function
compliant:
// Compliant: using standard NULL without redefining
char *ptr = NULL;

checks.message: Names of well-known C standard library macros and functions should be avoided as identifiers. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
